title: Renaissance movement begins
subtitle: Art movement
fact: The Renaissance is a European period of history and cultural movement, very roughly defined as covering the 14th through 17th centuries, though sometimes more narrowly defined for instance as only covering the 15th through 16th centuries.

title: Futurism movement begins
subtitle: Art movement
fact: Futurism was an artistic and social movement that originated in Italy, and to a lesser extent in other countries, in the early 20th century.

title: Romanticism movement begins
subtitle: Art movement
fact: Romanticism was an artistic and intellectual movement that originated in Europe towards the end of the 18th century.

title: Art Deco movement begins
subtitle: Art movement
fact: Art Deco, short for the French Arts décoratifs, is a style of visual arts, architecture, and product design that first appeared in Paris in the 1910s just before World War I and flourished internationally during the 1920s to early 1930s, through styling and design of the exterior and interior of anything from large structures to small objects, including clothing, fashion, and jewelry.

title: Art Nouveau movement begins
subtitle: Art movement
fact: Art Nouveau, Jugendstil in German, is an international style of art, architecture, and applied art, especially the decorative arts.

title: Impressionism movement begins
subtitle: Art movement
fact: Impressionism was a 19th-century art movement characterized by visible brush strokes, open composition, emphasis on accurate depiction of light in its changing qualities, ordinary subject matter, unusual visual angles, and inclusion of movement as a crucial element of human perception and experience.

title: Surrealism movement begins
subtitle: Art movement
fact: Surrealism is an art and cultural movement that developed in Europe in the aftermath of World War I in which artists aimed to allow the unconscious mind to express itself, often resulting in the depiction of illogical or dreamlike scenes and ideas.

title: Baroque movement begins
subtitle: Art movement
fact: The Baroque is a Western style of architecture, music, dance, painting, sculpture, poetry, and other arts that flourished from the early 17th century until the 1750s.

title: Bauhaus movement begins
subtitle: Art movement
fact: The Staatliches Bauhaus, commonly known as the Bauhaus, was a German art school operational from 1919 to 1933 that combined crafts and the fine arts.

title: Dada movement begins
subtitle: Art movement
fact: Dada or Dadaism was an international art movement that developed in the context of the Great War and Futurism first established in Zürich, Switzerland, and later quickly spread to Berlin, Paris, New York City and a variety of artistic centers in Europe and Asia.

title: Postmodernism movement begins
subtitle: Art movement
fact: Postmodernism encompasses a variety of artistic, cultural, and philosophical movements.

title: Rapping movement begins
subtitle: Art movement
fact: Rapping is an artistic form of vocal delivery and emotive expression that incorporates "rhyme, rhythmic speech, and [commonly] street vernacular".

title: Modernism movement begins
subtitle: Art movement
fact: Modernism was an early 20th-century movement in literature, visual arts, performing arts, and music that emphasized experimentation, abstraction, and subjective experience.

title: Ukiyo-e movement begins
subtitle: Art movement
fact: Ukiyo-e (浮世絵) is a genre of Japanese art that flourished from the 17th through 19th centuries.

title: Cubism movement begins
subtitle: Art movement
fact: Cubism is an early-20th-century avant-garde art movement which began in Paris.

title: Pre-Raphaelite Brotherhood movement begins
subtitle: Art movement
fact: The Pre-Raphaelite Brotherhood (PRB), later known as the Pre-Raphaelites, was a group of English painters, poets, and art critics, founded in 1848 by William Holman Hunt, John Everett Millais, Dante Gabriel Rossetti, William Michael Rossetti, James Collinson, Frederic George Stephens and Thomas Woolner who formed a seven-member "Brotherhood" partly modelled on the Nazarene movement.

title: Pop art movement begins
subtitle: Art movement
fact: Pop art is an art movement that emerged in the United Kingdom and the United States during the mid-to late 1950s.

title: Arts and Crafts movement movement begins
subtitle: Art movement
fact: The Arts and Crafts movement was an international trend in the decorative and fine arts that developed earliest and most fully in the British Isles and subsequently spread across the British Empire and to the rest of Europe and North America.

title: Expressionism movement begins
subtitle: Art movement
fact: Expressionism is a modernist movement, initially in poetry and painting, originating in Northern Europe around the beginning of the 20th century.

title: Neoclassicism movement begins
subtitle: Art movement
fact: Neoclassicism, also spelled Neo-classicism, emerged as a Western cultural movement in the decorative and visual arts, literature, theatre, music, and architecture that drew inspiration from the art and culture of classical antiquity.

title: Fauvism movement begins
subtitle: Art movement
fact: Fauvism is a style of painting and an art movement that emerged in France at the beginning of the 20th century.

title: Mannerism movement begins
subtitle: Art movement
fact: Mannerism is a style in European art that emerged in the later years of the Italian High Renaissance around 1520, spreading by about 1530 and lasting until about the end of the 16th century in Italy, when the Baroque style largely replaced it.

title: Fluxus movement begins
subtitle: Art movement
fact: Fluxus was an international, interdisciplinary community of artists, composers, designers, and poets during the 1960s and 1970s who, inspired by John Cage, engaged in experimental art performances which emphasized the artistic tradition of chance-based process over the finished product.

title: Modern art movement begins
subtitle: Art movement
fact: Modern art includes artistic work produced during the period extending roughly from the 1860s to the 1970s, and denotes the styles and philosophies of the art produced during that era.

title: Socialist realism movement begins
subtitle: Art movement
fact: Socialist realism, also known as socrealism, is a style of idealized realistic art that was developed in the Soviet Union and was the official cultural doctrine in that country between 1932 and 1988, as well as in other socialist countries after World War II.

title: Conceptual art movement begins
subtitle: Art movement
fact: Conceptual art, also referred to as conceptualism, is art in which the concept(s) or idea(s) involved in the work are prioritized equally to or more than traditional aesthetic, technical, and material concerns.

title: Post-impressionism movement begins
subtitle: Art movement
fact: Post-Impressionism was a predominantly French art movement which developed roughly between 1886 and 1905, from the last Impressionist exhibition to the birth of Fauvism.

title: Symbolism movement begins
subtitle: Art movement
fact: Symbolism was a late 19th-century art movement of French and Belgian origin in poetry and other arts seeking to represent absolute truths symbolically through language and metaphorical images, mainly as a reaction against naturalism and realism.

title: Constructivism movement begins
subtitle: Art movement
fact: Constructivism is an early twentieth-century art movement founded in 1915 by Vladimir Tatlin and Alexander Rodchenko.

title: De Stijl movement begins
subtitle: Art movement
fact: De Stijl was a Dutch art movement founded in 1917 by a group of artists and architects based in Leiden, Voorburg and Laren.

title: Vienna Secession movement begins
subtitle: Art movement
fact: The Vienna Secession is an art movement, adjacent to Art Nouveau, and that was formed in 1897 by a group of formidably talented Austrian painters, graphic artists, sculptors and architects, including Josef Hoffman, Koloman Moser, Otto Wagner and Gustav Klimt.

title: Classicism movement begins
subtitle: Art movement
fact: Classicism, in the arts, refers generally to a high regard for a classical period, classical antiquity in the Western tradition, as setting standards for taste which the classicists seek to emulate.

title: Gothic art movement begins
subtitle: Art movement
fact: Gothic art was a style of medieval art that developed in Northern France out of Romanesque art in the 12th century, led by the concurrent development of Gothic architecture.

title: Hip hop culture movement begins
subtitle: Art movement
fact: Hip-hop culture is an art movement that emerged in New York City, in the borough of the Bronx, primarily within the black community.

title: Japonisme movement begins
subtitle: Art movement
fact: Japonisme is a French term that refers to the popularity and influence of Japanese art and design among a number of Western European artists in the nineteenth century following the forced reopening of foreign trade with Japan in 1858.

title: Suprematism movement begins
subtitle: Art movement
fact: Suprematism is an early 20th-century art movement focused on the fundamentals of geometry, painted in a limited range of colors.

title: Op art movement begins
subtitle: Art movement
fact: Op art, short for optical art, is a style of visual art that uses distorted or manipulated geometrical patterns, often to create optical illusions.

title: Carolingian Renaissance movement begins
subtitle: Art movement
fact: The Carolingian Renaissance was the first of three medieval renaissances, a period of cultural activity in the Carolingian Empire.

title: Romanesque art movement begins
subtitle: Art movement
fact: Romanesque art is the art of Europe from approximately 1000 AD to the rise of the Gothic style in the 12th century, or later depending on region.

title: Die Brücke movement begins
subtitle: Art movement
fact: Die Brücke, also known as Künstlergruppe Brücke or KG Brücke, was a group of German expressionist artists formed in Dresden in 1905.
